headline_draft: Anthropic ships Claude Tag on Slack—AI teammate with shared context, multiplayer workflows, and 65% of Anthropic's own product code already written by it
summary: Anthropic launched Claude Tag on June 23, 2026—a persistent AI teammate that lives inside Slack channels rather than a separate chat window. Unlike Claude Code (which works for individual developers), Claude Tag operates as a shared team resource: anyone in a channel can tag @Claude to delegate tasks, the AI breaks work into steps, executes autonomously, and delivers results back in a thread that teammates can pick up and continue. Anthropic reports 65% of its product team's code is already generated by the internal version. The feature includes ambient mode (Claude proactively flags updates and forgotten tasks), persistent memory (it builds context over conversations), and granular access controls (admins set which channels, tools, and data each Claude instance can reach). Runs on Claude Opus 4.8. Currently in beta for Claude Enterprise and Team customers, with plans to expand beyond Slack.
why_pm: For PMs, Claude Tag represents the shift from AI as a solo productivity tool to AI as a team-embedded collaborator. This changes how product teams can delegate research, metric analysis, prototyping, and async work—freeing PMs to focus on judgment calls rather than execution. The 65% internal adoption rate at Anthropic (Anthropic's own product team uses it for most code) is the strongest validation signal yet of what an enterprise AI agent looks like in daily use.
key_facts: Launched June 23, 2026 in beta for Claude Enterprise and Team customers
Lives in Slack channels; multiplayer access with single shared Claude identity per channel
Anthropic reports 65% of its product team's code generated by internal Claude Tag
Features: async task execution, persistent channel memory, ambient proactive updates, granular access controls by channel/tool/data
Replaces existing Claude in Slack app; migration window ends August 3, 2026
Powers Claude Code and Cowork integration into team workflow
Runs on Opus 4.8; administrators control spend limits and audit logs
quotes: We see Claude Tag as an evolution of Claude Code. Claude Code, Cowork, and chat are very single-player, whereas Claude Tag is built to be interactive and multiplayer. When Claude Tag works in a channel, everyone can see it, and everyone can jump in, engage, and steer it in the right direction. — Cat Wu, Anthropic Head of Product for Claude Code

The structural change matters more than the feature list. The Claude Code team has been shipping with Claude Tag internally all year. It now writes 65% of our product team's code, including most of what built Claude Tag itself. — Anthropic (@ClaudeDevs)

headline_draft: Google delays Gemini 3.5 Pro past June—prediction markets now price July release odds higher, leaving Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 as the June frontier models
summary: Google promised Gemini 3.5 Pro in June 2026 at I/O (May 19), but as of June 24 it remains in limited enterprise preview only. Sundar Pichai's on-stage "give us until next month" has stretched: the model is still not in the consumer Gemini app, Google AI Studio, or general API as a stable release. Insider reports now suggest a July 2026 launch, with prediction markets showing only 0.5–4.5% odds for late-June release dates (June 24, 26, 30) and shifted focus to July. When Pro does ship, it will bring a 2-million-token context window (largest deployed in any frontier model), Deep Think reasoning mode (extended thinking, gated to Ultra subscribers), and multimodal understanding. Pro is positioned to recover from Gemini 3.5 Flash's known weakness: Flash outscores the previous Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding and agentic benchmarks but regresses on hard reasoning—the gap Pro is built to close.
why_pm: For product teams evaluating frontier models for June product roadmaps, Gemini 3.5 Pro's delay means the June decision is effectively between Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5. That changes the trade-off: you lose Google's context window advantage and have to choose between Anthropic's coding/agent reliability (Claude Code) or OpenAI's ecosystem and multimodal depth (GPT-5.5). PMs building long-horizon agent workflows or handling massive codebases should expect to re-evaluate when Pro finally ships in July. For now, test both Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 on your actual tasks rather than waiting.
key_facts: Announced May 19, 2026 at Google I/O with June general availability target
As of June 24, 2026: still in limited Vertex enterprise preview, not in public Gemini app or API
Insider reports suggest July 2026 launch; prediction markets at 0.5–4.5% for late-June dates
Specs when shipped: 2M-token context window (double 3.5 Flash), Deep Think reasoning (Ultra-tier only), multimodal support
Expected pricing: ~$15/$60 per 1M tokens (vs. Flash at $1.50/$9)
Known gap: Gemini 3.5 Flash beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on Terminal-Bench 2.1, MCP Atlas, and Finance Agent v2 (coding/agents) but regresses on hard reasoning; Pro is built to close that gap
Pro replaces Gemini Ultra tier use cases; Flash is the public 3.5 model until Pro launches
quotes: Give us until next month to get it to you. — Sundar Pichai, Google CEO, at I/O 2026 (drew audible groans from audience)

As of June 23, 2026, Gemini 3.5 Pro is in limited preview for select Vertex AI enterprise customers only. It has not shipped to the public Gemini app, Google AI Studio, or the consumer Gemini subscription.
